statement.headerDoc:
STEP Lehmer — Lehmer's totient conjecture small-n verification
  ==============================================================

  Conjecture (Lehmer, 1932, OPEN for 94 years):
    ∀ n ≥ 2, φ(n) | (n − 1) ⟹ n is prime.

  Empirically verified up to n < 10^30 (Cohen/Hagis 2011 et al.). No Lehmer
  number (composite n with φ(n) | n-1) has ever been found; necessary
  conditions include n odd, squarefree, ≥ 14 prime factors.

  This file gives a Mathlib-native Lean 4 verification for n ∈ [2, 1000]:
    - Every n with φ(n) | (n-1) in this range is prime.
    - Every composite in this range has φ(n
